sanskrit:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते॥
transliteration: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve ca sad ity etat prayujyate | praśaste karmaṇi tathā sac-chabdaḥ pārtha yujyate ||
literal_meaning: In the sense of existential reality (sat-bhāve) and in the mood of saintly intention (sādhu-bhāve) the syllable “sat” is employed; likewise, O Pārtha, the sound “sat” is applied to any action that is genuinely auspicious.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the word “sat” is the psychological anchor which converts ordinary action into self-transcending action; it signals alignment with reality rather than egoic fantasy.
psychological_state: Post-decision clarity—Arjuna now possesses a simple cognitive marker (“sat”) to test whether his choices are integrity-based or merely compliant.
trigger_scenario: Before pressing “send” on a job-resignation email you silently ask: "Is this sat—real and benevolent?" The word becomes an internal reality-check.
anchor_text: Let every deed be rooted in truth and goodwill.
